venue: Westmoreland Country Club, 7100 Mellon Rd, Export, PA 15632

ads.title: Brand introduction image
ads.primary_text:
Confluence Financial Partners — Proud Wealth Management Partner of the Pittsburgh Steelers — is hosting a complimentary evening at Westmoreland Country Club.

On Tuesday, May 5, our CEO, President, Chief Investment Officer, and Director of Wealth Planning are sitting down for an honest conversation about the financial decisions that matter most right now. Estate planning. The economy. Charitable giving. The pitfalls families don't see coming. Technology that actually helps.

Cocktails and hors d'oeuvres included. No follow-up calls. No obligation.

Seating is limited. Reserve your seat.
ads.headline: An Evening of Clarity
ads.hook: The Steelers' wealth management partner is hosting an evening for Pittsburgh families.

**Photo direction:** Branded office photo — Brandon across table from a couple (60s), natural conversation posture, warm overhead lighting. Conference room setting. If no Brandon meeting photo available, use stock-website couple reviewing documents with advisor.

**Text overlay:** ~10% coverage. Headline in bottom third over gradient. Logo small top-center.

**Gradient:** Bottom-up — transparent at 40%, dark navy 65% opacity at 100%.

ads.title: Educational carousel (5 cards)
ads.primary_text:
On Tuesday, May 5 at Westmoreland Country Club, four of our firm's leaders are sitting down together for a panel discussion — and you're invited.

Our CEO, President, CIO, and Director of Wealth Planning will cover six topics families should be thinking about right now: your financial future, estate planning and family meetings, charitable giving, the common pitfalls families face, the global economy, and how technology can tie it all together.

Complimentary cocktails and hors d'oeuvres. Seating is limited.
ads.headline: What Our Leadership Panel Covers
ads.hook: Four firm leaders. Six conversations. One evening at Westmoreland Country Club.

cards.label: Hook
cards.caption: Your financial plan touches a lot of moving pieces — investments, estate documents, taxes, family goals. Most of the time, nobody's looking at all of them together. That's what this evening is about.

cards.label: Economy & Portfolio
cards.caption: What does the current economy mean for your portfolio? Our Chief Investment Officer, Bill Winkeler, CFA, CFP, shares a forward-looking perspective on what families should be watching.

cards.label: Estate & Family Meetings
cards.caption: When was the last time your family sat down to talk about the plan? Estate documents get drafted and filed. Life changes. We'll discuss what a productive family meeting actually looks like.

cards.label: Charitable Giving & Pitfalls
cards.caption: Charitable giving can be one of the most rewarding — and most misunderstood — parts of a financial plan. We'll also cover the common pitfalls we see families make, and how to avoid them.

cards.label: CTA
cards.caption:
Tuesday, May 5 at 6:00 PM
Westmoreland Country Club, Export, PA
Complimentary cocktails and hors d'oeuvres.
Seating is limited. Reserve yours.

ads.title: Personal invite video
ads.production: CEO (Greg Weimer) or President (Gregory Weimer) direct-to-camera, filmed in office. Lapel mic. Captions mandatory.
ads.primary_text:
We host events like this because the most valuable conversations happen when people feel comfortable — not in a conference room, not on a sales call. Over cocktails, at a nice venue, with the people who actually run the firm.

On Tuesday, May 5, our CEO, President, CIO, and Director of Wealth Planning are hosting an evening at Westmoreland Country Club. We'll cover the economy, estate planning, charitable giving, and the questions families should be asking right now.

Complimentary. No follow-up afterward. We'd be glad to meet you.
ads.headline: A Personal Invitation
ads.hook: The most valuable conversations happen when people feel comfortable.
ads.video_script:
1. Open with WHY this event exists: "We host these events because the best conversations happen when people are comfortable — not in a conference room."
2. Middle: "On May 5, our entire leadership team — myself, our President, our CIO, and our Director of Wealth Planning — are hosting an evening at Westmoreland Country Club. Cocktails, good food, and an honest conversation about the financial topics that matter right now."
3. Close: "Complimentary, no follow-up. We'd be glad to meet you there."

**Play button:** Large centered circle overlay (standard platform play icon) signals this is video content.

ads.title: Peace of mind image
ads.primary_text:
You're in the years where the decisions compound the most. The moves you make now with your portfolio, your estate, your tax strategy — they shape the next 20 years.

The question most families don't ask until later: are all those pieces actually working together?

On Tuesday, May 5, our leadership team is hosting a complimentary evening at Westmoreland Country Club. Cocktails, conversation, and a panel on the six topics that matter most to families in the Pittsburgh area right now.

No follow-up calls. No obligation. Seating is limited.
ads.headline: Clarity Starts with Conversation
ads.hook: You're in the years where the decisions compound the most.
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1080 (square) | **Placement:** Feed
**Layout:** Photo-dominant (Archetype 1) — matches inspiration Ads 01/02 most closely

```
COMPOSITION:
+--------------------------------------+
|  LOGO (top-center, navy)             |
|                                      |
|  [Lifestyle photo: couple (60s)      |
|   walking on Naples beach at         |
|   golden hour. Relaxed, arms         |
|   linked, looking at water.          |
|   Warm tones, open sky above.]       |
|                                      |
|  "Confidence in                      |
|   What You've Built."                |
|  (Ivypresto, dark navy, centered     |
|   — positioned in open sky area)     |
|                                      |
|  [SIGN UP] (outlined, navy)          |
|                                      |
+--------------------------------------+
```

**Photo direction:** This ad mirrors the inspiration directly — lifestyle couple in warm outdoor Naples setting. Key: natural light, relaxed body language, Gulf/beach/waterfront visible. Text overlays onto open sky area (no gradient needed if sky provides contrast). Logo in navy (not white) since background is light.

**This is the emotional anchor ad.** The photo should feel aspirational but real — "this is your life, we help you protect it."

**Photo/video direction:** Naples montage — warm, golden, community feel. No advisor in frame. The city IS the subject. Gulf at golden hour, couples walking Mercato, Third Street boutiques, waterfront dining. Mix 3-4 shots in 15 seconds. Text overlays appear/dissolve with each scene transition.

**Key visual difference from other ads:** No faces, no advisor — this is about PLACE and IDENTITY. The viewer sees their own life reflected.

ads.title: Infographic / visual concept
ads.primary_text:
There's a real difference between having a financial plan and having all the pieces working together.

With coordination: your estate documents are current, your portfolio is aligned to the economy, your family has had the conversation, and your charitable giving is structured thoughtfully.

Without it: advisors in separate silos, estate documents gathering dust, family guessing at intentions, and market reactions instead of a strategy.

On Tuesday, May 5, our CEO, President, CIO, and Director of Wealth Planning are hosting a complimentary evening at Westmoreland Country Club. Six panel topics. Cocktails and hors d'oeuvres. Seating is limited.
ads.headline: Coordinated vs. Fragmented
ads.hook: There's a real difference between having a plan and having all the pieces working together.
ads.infographic_direction:
Left column, "Coordinated":
- Forward-looking strategy reviewed regularly
- Family meetings happening — everyone aligned
- Estate documents current and working together
- Charitable giving structured for impact and tax benefit
- Portfolio positioned for the current economy
- Technology connecting the financial planning pieces

Right column, "Fragmented":
- Reacting to markets without a forward view
- Family conversations put off or avoided
- Estate documents outdated or conflicting
- Charitable intentions unfulfilled
- Portfolio disconnected from the broader plan
- Advisors and documents in separate silos
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1080 (square) | **Placement:** Feed
**Layout:** Split comparison (custom — not directly from archetypes)

```
COMPOSITION:
+--------------------------------------+
|  LOGO (top-center, white)            |
|                                      |
+------------------+-------------------+
|  COORDINATED     |  WITHOUT          |
|  (gold accent)   |  COORDINATION     |
|  (Rival Sans,    |  (white 60%)      |
|  label)          |  (Rival Sans)     |
|                  |                   |
|  [Dark navy      |  [Darker navy     |
|   #152d4b]       |   #0b1a2e]        |
|                  |                   |
|  ✓ Trusted       |  ✗ Court-appointed|
|    decision-     |    decision-      |
|    makers named  |    makers         |
|                  |                   |
|  ✓ Roth timed    |  ✗ Tax timing     |
|    with tax      |    works against  |
|    picture       |    you            |
|                  |                   |
|  ✓ Estate docs   |  ✗ Outdated or    |
|    up to date    |    conflicting    |
|                  |    documents      |
|  ✓ Heirs         |                   |
|    prepared      |  ✗ Family left    |
|                  |    guessing       |
|  ✓ Charitable    |                   |
|    giving        |  ✗ Intentions     |
|    structured    |    unfulfilled    |
|                  |                   |
+------------------+-------------------+
|                                      |
|  [SIGN UP] (outlined, white)         |
|  Wed, May 14 | Naples                |
+--------------------------------------+
```

**Design direction:** Two-column comparison on solid navy. Left column (coordinated) uses gold checkmarks (#c9a84c) and slightly lighter navy background. Right column (uncoordinated) uses white/red ✗ marks and darker navy. Thin vertical divider line (white, 1px, 30% opacity) separates columns.

**Diagonal line texture:** Lines-02.png at 5% opacity across the full background — adds brand texture to what would otherwise be a flat navy card.

**Typography:** Ivypresto for the column headers ("Coordinated Plan" / "Without Coordination"). Rival Sans Light for the bullet points. All white text.

**This is the one ad that breaks the photo-dominant pattern.** The infographic comparison IS the visual. Keep it clean, readable at phone size. Max 5 items per column.

ads.production: CIO Bill Winkeler at desk or conference room. Conversational, not scripted. Lapel mic. Captions mandatory.
ads.primary_text:
The economy is giving families a lot to think about right now. Interest rates, market volatility, what it means for the portfolio you've been building for years.

Our Chief Investment Officer, Bill Winkeler, CFA, CFP, is one of four panelists at our complimentary evening on Tuesday, May 5 at Westmoreland Country Club. He'll cover the current trends in the global economy and what families should be considering for their portfolios.

Cocktails and hors d'oeuvres included. Seating is limited.

**Design direction:** This is the ONE text-forward ad in the campaign. No photo. The scarcity message IS the visual — "8 Seats Left" dominates the frame. Dark navy background with the diagonal line texture adds brand identity without distracting. Gold accent color used for the divider line and CTA border — the only ad that uses gold prominently. This signals urgency within the brand system.

**Why no photo:** The number is the scroll-stopper. A photo would dilute the urgency. Inspiration precedent: PPC Ad 03 uses a similar text-heavy layout on navy background.

ads.title: Final reminder video
ads.production: iPhone selfie-style from CEO (Greg Weimer). Raw, personal, authentic. No graphics, no music. Captions added in post. Should NOT look like an ad.

1. "Hey, just a quick note. Our evening at Westmoreland Country Club this Tuesday is almost full."
2. "A few seats left. Complimentary cocktails, great conversation, no strings."
3. "Bring your spouse if you can. Would be glad to meet you. Link below."
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1920 (vertical) | **Placement:** Stories, Reels
**Layout:** Raw/authentic — NO designed template

```
STORIES/REELS:
+--------------------+
| [Progress bar]     |
|                    |
| [iPhone selfie     |
|  video: Brandon    |
|  holding phone,    |
|  natural light,    |
|  office or home    |
|  background.       |
|  NO graphics.      |
|  NO music.         |
|  NO brand overlay. |
|  Auto-generated    |
|  captions only.]   |
|                    |
| "This Wednesday    |
|  | A few seats     |
|  left"             |
| (Platform auto-    |
|  caption style,    |
|  NOT designed      |
|  overlay)          |
|                    |
| [SIGN UP]          |
| (platform CTA      |
|  sticker only)     |
+--------------------+
```

**Photo/video direction:** This ad intentionally looks NOTHING like the other 8. It's a raw iPhone selfie — Brandon picks up his phone, talks for 10-15 seconds, puts it down. Natural lighting. No branded elements visible (or incidental at most). No music. The only "design" is platform-generated captions.

**Why raw:** This is the authenticity play. After 3 weeks of polished branded ads, the pattern interrupt of a real person talking directly to you is the final conversion nudge. It should NOT look like an ad. If a viewer's first thought is "this is a real person talking to me," the creative is working.

**Do NOT add:** Logo overlay, brand colors, headline text, CTA button rendered into the image, music, or any post-production graphics. The platform CTA sticker is the only designed element.
